Title: Usage of ECC QA (FC0) for SCP R2 SIT and ECC Bench (FB0) for UAT1
Description: In Q3'24, the SCP program brought requirements for ECC testing env after IAO ERP timeline was in place. Due to SCP's need for an integrated ECC env for its SIT test cycle, SCP/Infra agreed to designate ECC QA (FC0) for SCP SIT. Another requirement brought in by SCP was the need for a legacy Enterprise HANA mock test env, but that required too much effort/complexity to setup within program timelines. Therefore, the teams worked a plan to designate legacy SAP Benchmark (which includes FB0) to be used for SCP BIT test cycle and align that with ERP R2 UAT1.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
The decision to use FC0 for SCP SIT requires an out of cycle QA refresh. This was carried out in WW50 2024.

The decision to use FB0 for both SCP BIT and ERP R2 UAT1 requires the following:
1. WW09 2025 Benchmark Refresh (which requires a WW08.0 data capture). Both are on track.
2. Since FB0 is NOT an integrated environment like FC0, both SCP and B-Apps teams need to assess their R2 test requirements and mitigate accordingly. For those scenarios that require connection to FC0, teams can work with ERP Test Team and ITAIS Middleware to plan a strategy to switch between ECC environments as needed.

Title: FPR PC&IV: SKF Equipped Capacity by Volume (Front End)  at dot process level - Delay in providing transformation logic for the SKF is impacting design & build sprints.
Description:
The FPR transformation logic for Statistical Key Figure (SKF) Equipped Capacity by Volume (Front End),  must be defined field by field. Delays in delivering this transformation logic are significantly impacting the ERP implementation’s Design and Build sprints. If unresolved, these delays may disrupt the overall project timeline.

System Details: 
- System: Wafer Starts Per Week (WSPW) [B-App ID 4119]  
- Target System: ECA-SCP-IF Zone. 
- Source Tower Team: ECA-SCP
- RICEFW FPRI0554

Business Context & Impact:
The equipped capacity needs to be derived as an SKF driver based on the factory's production capability. This dataset must include the volume of wafers manufactured in a given period, with data structured at the dot process level for front-end MFG. The dot process level must have a 1:1 mapping with production planning work centers.

SKF drivers are essential for cost allocation from the primary cost center to secondary cost centers, which is critical for the costing model. Without the transformation logic in place, current product costing project timeline is disrupted.

RAID History:
This RAID item replaces the previously closed RAID 2736 (closed January 2025) to ensure clarity by specifying the system and SKF data element requirements.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
3/6/25 Bala completed transformational logic and FS. Deloitte approved to close this RAID as "Complete".

3/3/25 PMO connected with FPR on 3/3. The due date of 2/14/2025 will not change to bring visibility to the missing transformation logic issue and delay from system teams. The delay is due to the misaligned design level of details and timeline deliverables for SCP & ERP programs. The FPR team provided an estimated close date of 3/07/25.

2/25/25 Sajiv provided an overview of System architecture with SKF structure. AR: Bala to complete CC mapping. 

2/20/25 Ale & Sajiv work on transformation logic mapping. 

2/13/25 Preliminary data structure was provided by FTS IF. FPR Deloitte confirm that this RAID can be closed or identify any opens.

2/6/25 Amy's and Vasu's data elements are clear. This capability will land as part of R2 in PROD. The data structure is available. FPR will do the mapping in ECA view wit Vasu's help.

Title: PC&IV: Intel Foundry:  A summary of Actual Costing Calculation & Material Ledger Processes
Description:
"  1. Inventory recorded at Standard Cost during months
     During each month, system posts FG Inventory and WIP at standard cost as follows:
     FG Inventory Value =  Standard Cost * Actual Quantity of wafers manufactured during current month. 
     WIP Value = Standard Cost * Actual Quantity of WIP wafers started this month. 
				 
  2. Month-End Actual Cost Adjustment
     The adjustments will be posted to the same inventory GLs used for Standard Cost. 
     A. During the last day of every month (M1, M2, M3 LWD) , the system completes month-end actual cost, and automatically posts  adjustment for FG Inventory and WIP based on the Actual Cost as follows:         
        Month-end adjustment: 
	      FG Inventory Value = (Standard Cost- Actual Cost)* Actual Quantity of wafers in Inventory this month. 
	      WIP Value = (Standard Cost- Actual Cost)* Actual Quantity of WIP wafers started this month.
			 
     B. During the first day of the following month (M1, M2, M3 WD0), the system automatically reverses the month-end adjustment posted  during M1, M2, M3 LWD.
	   
  3. Quarterly Standard Cost Re-evaluation.
New standard cost will be calculated based on latest forecast spending.
During the first day of the following month (M1, M2, M3 WD0), the system will post Standard Cost revaluation entry as follows:			
	      Inventory Dr / Cr  ( Old Std Cost – New Std Cost ) * Inventory Qty 
	      Revaluation adjustment Dr/ Cr "

Title: As of Q1’26, SAP S/4 systems will be 3 years behind latest SAP software levels, introducing risk of missing latest fixes, patches and features
Description:
The SAP Maintenance Release (MR) is a yearly 20-week effort where platform/development/business resources test/deploy the latest SAP application fixes, patches, capabilities. Default scope is all SAP products. The goal is to remain healthy and provide capabilities ahead of business needs.  HANA patches applied separately from MR on a quarterly basis.
MR was cancelled in 2025 due to impact to IAO ERP R1 and R2 development and testing.
As of late 2024, functional teams are asking about new capabilities. Expect this to continue.
By R3 go-live, we will have been 3 years behind the latest SAP software levels.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
Next window for MR would be after ERP R2 (Sep.'25) but before ERP R3 integrated test start (Feb.’26) ~ 20-week span.

3/21/25: Infra horizontal to announce to ERP PMO Weekly Leads that unless there are clear objections, no MR will be scheduled until after R3

4/8/25 - Since there are no broad compelling drivers for any S/4 updates and due to the disruption to the ERP program. that would be caused, we are deciding no MR until after R3. Closing this RAID.

Title: Implementation of Separate AGS Job Roles for Non-Production and Production SAP Environments
Description: It was agreed to establish AGS job roles for both non-production and production environments where applicable. SAP Non-production roles will include specific identifiers (SAP Test Systems Only - ) within AGS to indicate that they are for testing purposes only. These roles will be limited to non-production SAP access and will not include boundary system access. In contrast, roles that provision to production systems will not have specific identifiers and may include bundled access to boundary applications if applicable for that job role.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
2/19 - Team continued discussion on this decision. There is an action item to confirm the non production identifier with security champions
2/26 - Non production identifier not confirmed, action to schedule follow up call

Title: ILM to SAP Master data updates
Description:
Three RICEFW are planned to update the material master from ILM to MDG/S4
1.	2819 - ILM to MDG– QM activation/deactivation in material master
2.	2756 (PTPI1153) - ILM to SAP S/4 – Create/Modify QIR 
3.	2757 (PTPI1153) - ILM to SAP S/4 – Material assignment to Inspection Plan
The RICEFW PTPI1153 and PTPI1153 are approved and will be taken up for development by the PTP Team. These two RICEFWs will be ready for Release 3. However the first RICEFW (2819) which is to be built from ILM to MDG will only be built and available for Release 4. This RICEFW is owned by the MDG team and during the time gap between Release 3 and Release 4 Go-Lives these will be a process put in place to manually update the data within MDG. This RAID item is to finalize the process which will be used for manually updating the master data within MDG. The process should take into account the lead time required to update the data in MDG and who all will be involved as stakeholders to communicate and finally make the updates within MDG.

3/27 - Lisa: Change Request approved

Title: SMG: MySamples Design/build opens due to Billback elimination
Description:
•	FPR & PTP have set a goal of eliminating billbacks by R3
•	Currently the former IVE team uses My Samples to collect system demand from other validation teams, they then place bulk purchase orders for the systems with ODMs and do billbacks at end of the month to recover the costs
•	FPR wants to capture all the PO details (like WBS and Cost Center) in My Samples so those other validation teams can be billed directly
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
5/27: Closing this RAID item about Billback elimination and has opened a new RAID item on overall MySamples design #1305, which is delayed by more than a month now and has put the Boundary app development at risk
5/15: Design for Samples was closed as of WW19 and then early this week (WW20) design was reopened in terms of MRP check not happening. So, team is back to square that we don't have finalized design approach and therefore, we don't have sizing for Samples. It is also eating into crucial development window for Samples team to complete development by end of the year. There is no ETA for design closure at this moment. However, if I don't get a reasonable ETA by WW21.1, I'm going to raise severity of this RAID
4/25: design closed but build plans to be finalized by end of WW19; trying to avoid build extending into 2026
4/24: What was originally raised as a problem statement/risk around billback elimination has evolved into addressing MySamples current design/biz process gaps with the e2e industry standard solution proposed in collaboration with FTS, PTP, OTC and FPR towers; plan to close MySamples design by end of WW18 with build schedule
4/11 - In last Friday’s meeting Deloitte, Tower Leads, and IAO End-to-End team concluded that the current approved design where My Samples would need to connect to S/4 to create Purchase Requisitions (PR) has flaws and is not the path we should take forward.
Alternative designs being considered are (1) for IVE team to manually create the PRs or  (2) something similar to today’s process where My Samples triggers MRP to create PRs.  Sekhar and Deloitte team is analyzing the plants used in today’s ZISO / MRP process to see if similar automation is feasible for R3. 
Joaquim Guedes is leading the discussion on what is the best design for Intel to follow when creating a PO with an ODM, handling inventory of parts we receive from them, and distributing those parts to labs/individual teams without the use of billbacks.
We will only know what changes are needed from My Samples once the above designs are finalized. NO ETA on this
3/17: Team already working on non-ideal option 1, while there is a bigger group meeting scheduled between FTS, FPR and OTC tower reps on WW12.2. Option 2 is what FTS wants
3/11: Two options were proposed (see slides 3 & 4)
•	Option #1 does not align to FTS recommended MRP process (skips MRP) but allows for automatically copying WBS and CC into the PO
•	Option #2 follows FTS recommended process and will make it easier to transition My Samples to ALPS
•	Currently there is pressure for Samples to Complete the design for #1, but we are in the process of escalating this to leadership (Biz & IT) to seek approval to switch to #2

Title: SMG: Customer planning hierarchy is supporting top 30 customers for demand and is missing out on Revenue customers - impacting SMG/Rebates apps
Description:
The Demand/Supply teams are advocating for a reduction in the number of customers they plan demand for, targeting around 30. However, this change would exclude several customers essential for revenue forecast  business processes, as we have direct revenue and rebate relationships with customers different from those we invoice.
Impact:
1.	Revenue Management: A new customer hierarchy would need to be maintained in Profisee, separate from the enterprise master data. This would result in duplicate processes across two tools, leading to inefficiencies and poor design.
2.	Downstream Applications: All downstream applications from revenue would need to adopt this new customer hierarchy instead of the enterprise hierarchy. Affected applications include: 1) All SMG revenue processes, such as RevOpt and POR 2) SMG analytics 3) KPIs 4)SAC

Title: Query on fetching Supplier Batch number field
Description:
The ‘Supplier Batch number’ is required to be saved within the ‘SAP Batch master’. This field is required from MQCS perspective as all dispositions within MQCS are managed with ‘Supplier Batch number’. There were two options discussed during the PTP sub-team meeting on 03/13/2025 (listed below) -
 
Option 1 – Save the ‘Supplier Batch Number’ within the Batch master (Basic data 1 view – Field LICHA). This field can hold upto 15 characters.
Option 2 – Save the ‘Supplier Batch Number’ within the Batch master (Classification view – Custom characteristic). This field can hold upto 30 characters.
 
The SAP recommendation and business preference is to go ahead with Option 1. However for Option 1 – The business needs to confirm if ‘Supplier Batch number’ from all Suppliers can be within 15 characters. If this is not possible then the back up is to pursue option 2.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
4/1: Had discussion with Lisa in QM pod call. 
Finalized Option is "Option 1"
If Supplier batch number is more than 15 characters, then MQCS to send upto 15 characters (If truncation is required – The first 15 characters will be sent)

Title: PTP team has requested access in S4 based on their previous ECC access
Description:
This is a security risk.
Why This is a Risk:
•	Increased Maintenance Complexity: Adding a large number of tcodes increases the complexity of maintenance.
•	Obsolete Requests: We have identified several instances where requested tcodes are obsolete, have been replaced with new functionality in S/4, or the functionality that was previously in ECC is now being handled by a new solution outside of S/4. The tower teams are not thoroughly vetting their requests before submitting them to us.
•	Unnecessary Access: Many of these requests lack a clear business justification and appear to be requested on a "just in case" basis. 
•	User Experience: Adding numerous tcodes, many of which are redundant, will negatively impact the user experience. Users may encounter duplicate Fiori apps, multiple apps for the same activity, and overall confusion about how to perform their tasks effectively in fiori.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
4/1 - FPR- Credit and collections team justified their access, mapped to L4s, and provided reasoning for their access. FPR team is providing governance on ECC requests. 
P2P- Enable payments, GSPO is the only remaining team outstanding
4/15 - Still working with PTP team to map the ECC tcodes to L1-L3. Tower team is also updating the list of tcodes to verify whether each one is required or not. Work is in progress
4/21 - PTP Deloitte tower to review the list of tcodes and determine which belong to PTP versus other towers. They will then map PTP tcodes to L1-L3.
4/22 - L1-L3 mapping is in progress targed by EOW (4/25)
4/22 - NL: Closing this RAID as the intent (teams requesting ECC access w/o requirements being mapped) is being addressed. The PTP tower has the action to map L1-L3 for the tcodes being requested.  For tcodes that cannot be mapped to a requirement, those need to be addressed through a change request.
Decision Made: PTP team is mapping L1-L3 to the ECC tcodes being requested.

Title: S&C - Infrastructure Security - Penetration Testing
Description:
– There is no identified penetration testing lead/POC from Intel. This may lead to lack of oversight, delayed or limited decision-making on/ ownership of the outcomes of the penetration testing efforts and therefore, no mitigation steps being undertaken.
Penetration testing of the three in-scope SAP systems (GRC, S/4 HANA, MDG) can take at least 6 weeks to complete which will be beyond the planned Go Live date of May Release1. If there is no change in this date, there is a risk of previously undiscovered vulnerabilities to be moved into production environments with potential impact to data confidentiality & integrity, and operational impact.
The toolset required to do the penetration testing should be approved and necessary provision made for pen testers to use Intel-owned applications like Burp Suite. Any delay in getting the approved set of tools will adversely impact the pen testing delivery timelines
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
Awaiting response from Intel team on the POC and approval to kick off the Penetration testing of the in-scope applications for Release1 and other prerequisites such tool set approval.

After conducting thorough due diligence, the teams have agreed that the risk associated with the R1/R2 go-live is low. Subsequently, the Intel Security team will revisit the controls assessment. 
Raj will be submitting a Change Request (CR) to officially document the descoping of Deloitte's deliverables for R1/R2. 
Additionally, we will work on creating a list of "pre-requisite details" that Deloitte will need to gather for the R3 Penetration Testing and Risk Assessment. 
This decision has been reviewed and approved by the respective teams involved

Title: BY is not accounting for non-viable wafers (100% DPW Hits) in Fab and Bump Stage. Risk to Expedite process
Description: RISK (logged by FSCO team) : In case material has a 100% DPW hit in Fab and Bump stages, the current FSCO and BY design will treat these wafers as completely valid WIP for commitment purposes. Consequently, for materials still in the Fab and Bump stages, BY will overestimate the available WIP supply. When addressing expedite requests based on the Planner Orders versus Customer Demand analysis in BY, we might promise to expedite materials that we cannot actually support. Since most of expedite requests involve material in the Fab and  Bump stages, this design poses a significant risk of failing to meet expedite requests after committing to them.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
7/8: Marking as closed - this item has been committed for R2 design/development by Ashish Nemani
04/01: IF FTS: Design has been closed; Educating Yacov on the design; No CCB for this Raid; Lorinda to close;
3/31: Surfaced by Yacov Torchinsky. Background: 
100%'s DPW hits are not viable, and will be accounted for in Sort. This is the correct approach 
Fab and Bump, however, are projecting these as viable. This poses a risk to the expedite process 
For Fab and Bump, it is critical to peg correctly to the viable values, not the total including 100% DPW hits
Assigned to Name(s): Dan, Lorinda, Madeline Slattery, Mona Kamath, Tushar Nachnani, Yacov

Title: DR Drill for R1 and R2
Description: As R1 is more of a technical go-live, and R1 boundary apps already have BC and DR plans in place, prior to R1 go-live, there will not be a coordinated "BCDR Drill" as Intel does yearly. Instead, the SAP platforms team will perform technical validation of the R1 SAP S/4 DR systems  such as application startup, connectivity to database, and RFC connection checks.  Both R1 SAP S/4 platform and R1 boundary apps product owners will update their BC/DR plans ahead of R1 go-live.  For R2, the annual Intel "BCDR Drill" will be pulled in from its normal timing of September to June to cover a full scope of R1, R2 and legacy applications to validate all applications BCDR plans.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
4/4/25 - Dates for the pulled in BCDR Drill are being proposed. Need an overall coordinator to PM the effort as IT Ops as dropped the role - RAID 00864 created for that
5/6/25 - This part of this KDD concerning the BCDR drill timing was reversed as of WW18 - the dates will be kept at normal annual drill timing and NOT pulled in to before R2.  Today, reps from Data, FPR, Master Data and Analytics in a meeting all agreed the above strategy and tests/results are valid.

Title: FPR PC&IV: ICOST Straddle through R5 - E2E Flows - R1/R2 Testing issues
Description:
The Issue:
The ICOST team doesn't have full set of data to conduct the comprehensive ICOST Straddle test for R1 and R2.
Different design options are still considered for R2. R2 has Inventory sites changes going live, about which the ICOST Straddle team was not timely informed .

The risk is that after R2, R3 and R4 go-live, ICOST would not be able to provide timely, complete and accurate Cost and Inventory data for Intel's Financial Reporting (Intel Foundry, Intel Product and Intel Corporate). Which would risk delay or failure of issuing of Intel's Financial Statements.  

ICOST Straddle testing is not included in the R1 & R2 end-to-end flow. As of today, we are behind on testing. It is not clear if the R2 ICOST design is complete related to R2 architecture and OTC data flow. We have assigned C&I execution team to complete the testing, and their availability is constrained by close support and other daily activities. 

ICOST will remain the system of record for Cost and Inventory Financial Reporting through R5 go-live, so it’s essential that we incorporate ICOST into the R2, R3, and R4 E2E test flows. Additionally, in the E2E flows for R3 and R4, we need to contemplate the Product Cost parallel run with and without ICOST.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
6/23<Kelly> : Closing because this is a duplicate of 1127.
5/28 Kelly Gerber Put back to P1 from P2, change due date to 6/20.  This risk cannot be reduced until a successful UAT2 test run
ICOST test scenarios and scripts have been included in E2E test flow.
5/23 Kelly Gerber
R2 CIBR/ICOST Update
•	CIBR/ICOST test has been reduced from P0 to P1 Risk status.  We are trending toward a P2 status as plans become finalized.
•	Full Cost testing not available until UAT2 due to data mismatches and new development 
•	Config/code is complete in CIBR/ICOST for new movement types and new plant codes.  A few batches were successfully tested in UAT1.
•	Product and Batch data is very limited for UAT2.  Test data has been identified for UAT2, and we are working with the E2E team to ensure this data goes through the right testing and is reserved for ICOST testing scenarios.
•	Note:  CATTS/TCA and ECD systems have also been struggling with lack of test data.  They can also use the same data identified for ICOST.
•	Cost regression test with ECC (IP) data is planned for UAT2.
Impacted Teams: B-Apps, FPR, FTS IF, Test Management

Title: Golden Data set to be used by ERP and SCP for E2E string tests for UAT2
Description:
In UAT 1 we had issues with testing as we did not have a single golden data set that will be used by ERP and SCP for all E2E and string tests for UAT2. Overall, we need a customer-to-material mapping for UAT2 test cases led by E2E team getting alignment across SCP, ERP, Towers, Horizontals for the golden data set. Below is an initial list of what is needed, but this is not all inclusive. For UAT2, the customer code view in ERP and SCP should follow the same strategy of masking/non-masking to bridge gaps in issue resolution.

1.	Material status as Active
2.	MM# price loaded in ECC
3.	Plant codes set up (validate in MPL)
4.	Routes are set up
5.	Exact sold-to/MM for B2B customers
6.	Product code to customer code match
7.	For IFS wafer sales and turnkey  active MM to have accurate capacity and BOM information in the test environment
8.	MRP Area extension
9.	Material attributes for compliance are set-up (COO, COF, COA, Customer Part#, Customer Lot, etc.)
10.	POLP sync w/ our dataset to ensure the examples are working correctly (SO Line items qty, MM#, etc.)
11.	MM validated in MPL environment we are using for UAT2. Some of the MMs we picked this round were not available in MPL CONS.
12.	MM, Plant, Quantity per sales order so we generate the right factory waybills

Title: ECA Single Tenant Three Data Lakes Super Zones
Description:
ECA Single Tenant Three Data lakes with IP, IF, and Intel Corp Super Zones.
The ECA data will be securely governed and maintained in three super zones, each with specific security access roles.
These super zones are pivotal for data segregation and the secure implementation of IAO guardrails.

Title: ECA Speed Layer
Description:
The speed layer will establish a unified ECA platform for near real-time (NRT) operational reports.
The speed layer involves building reports and dashboards directly on top of raw data. Metrics for these dashboards will be generated on the fly without any data persistence.
This Speed layer will handle limited, time-bound data, reflecting the source system (S/4) and containing only current data.

Title: ECA Azure DevOps Branching Strategy
Description:
Multiple teams can concurrently develop R1/R2/R3 multi-release objects within a single ECA development environment.
Feature  Main1  Release (ITC/UAT) branching strategy will be used for R1 and R2 releases. 
Feature  Main2  Release (ITC/UAT) branching strategy will be used for the R3 release. 
Code synchronization will occur periodically between the Main1 and Main2 branches. 
The Main1 and Main2 branches will eventually merge into the Main release branch for QA and PROD migration.

Title: Alfresco platform stability
Description: The Alfresco platform is experiencing stability issues in non-prod that are related to a high volume of files being generated by IAO SAP systems.  We found at least two examples of files from SAP being stored in Alfresco with no known business requirement.  In one example, SAP is generating the files as part of basic Workflow functionality.  These “event” xml files are related to MDG Business Partner creation.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
7/9: The SAP-recommended configuration changes are completed  to prevent unwanted XML file generation from Business Partner transactions. Initial testing confirms that no XML files are being generated.
6/6: This RAID is assigned to Technology Team ABAP, Please check with Mohan, Vasanth mohan <vasamohan@deloitte.com>, Kunjir, Vijay S <vijay.s.kunjir@intel.com> for updates. 
We need assistance from the Business Partner MDG team to determine what is creating these files and if they are required.  If not, disable the configuration. 
These are the MDG (Intel and DT) that we met with last week:
Ramirez Fallas, Diego;
Shah, Shruti;
rausingh@deloitte.com
Singh, RaunaqX;

As discussed in the meeting on 5/14, the issue is not related to MDG and looped in Tech team to look at the issue
Tech team update: We connected with Vijay Kunjir from Intel and analysed this issue. This looks like a configuration that needs to be adjusted so that data doesnt flow to Alfresco. We will need alfresco/Infra help to arrive at a solution

6/5 JohnC:  The SAP Infra team (Basis) is unable to help on any functional config change that would be required to eliminate this noise being produced by SAP and sent to Alfresco.  The fix is not in the SAP Content Mgmt config or the integration with Alfresco.  The MDG Business Partner functional team along with the Deloitte Tech team need to figure out how to change the BP configuration so these event xml documents are not created. Updating owner to Nisha and hope she can find help.

06/10: Tech team, MDG team and Intel IT had a joint working session to discuss the issue. Based on the discussion, we have planned to reach out to SAP for their insight on this. OSS notes number will be updated once its created.

6/18 JohnC - SAP Case 678304/2025 is created and IV is given a heads up. Based on statement from Alfresco platform team that they will not proceed w/ any R2 Prod integrations until this issue is resolved, I have updated this RAID to a P1.
6/18 JohnC - SAP Case 678304/2025 is created and IV is given a heads up. Based on statement from Alfresco platform team that they will not proceed w/ any R2 Prod integrations until this issue is resolved,. Need proper owner (MDG Business Partner or Technology or ?)

6/26 JohnC - SAP Case 678304/2025. SAP/Matt/Vijay/Vasanth have implemented the SAP-recommended configuration changes to prevent unwanted XML file generation from Business Partner transactions. Initial testing confirms that no XML files are being generated. Systems BI0-200/250 are now ready for end-to-end testing of this scenario.

7/1/25 JohnC - Lowering the severity to Medium as the team now has a good handle on how to identify and adjust config to control any flood of XML files sent to Alfresco. Also, Alfresco platform team is now comfortable to proceed with integrations when the time comes in preparation for R2 cutover/go-live.

07/9/25 - Vasanth Mohan - This issue has been fixed for MDG data replication into S/4 and Alfresco based on the SAP configuration steps given as part of SAP Case 678304/2025. Any other Alfresco related config issue can be tracked seperately.

Title: Pricing Configuration for Commercial Invoice on STO for WIP Movement
Description:
User stories for Pricing on commercial invoice for STOs to move WIP between IF plants have to be defined by business for Deloitte to work on the RICEFW. 
Currently there is no business lead driving these cross functional requirements. The pricing calculation is defined by Finance, Trade team provides content needed on CI, Procurement team owns the STO design for cross plant/cross company code stock transfer, and logistics own printing the CI to execute the WIP movement. We also do not have a Deloitte lead to coordinate all of this cross functional design and configuration. Shipments need price for CI.

Following scenarios need to be covered:
1.   Customer bailed material being shipped across STO – This will follow the ZBAI – condition records will be maintained by OTC team based on Customer info. Open on ownership
2.   Intel Products Bailed materials – It is not clear who will own BAI condition records. Open on the ownership
3.   Mid stage shipments: Using UNBW on STO for the corresponding UPI,  derive Pricing from the UPI standard cost to show on the CI
4. End –stage shipments: Standard STO with valuated UPI – There is a pricing procedure from Procure to pay for these, as was stated by Murthy from Deloitte.
5. TD Lots or Non-revenue lots – Ship UNBW for TD Engineering lots to be shipped. Pricing on the CI needs to be determined based on Stage – Need Finance input.
6. There is a scenario for CI when lots move within a plant in Kulim, between buildings, where an STO will be generated for  SLoc to SLoc movement. This was not discussed in the meeting, but needs to be supported. Requirements will be provided by KM business team.
7. Pricing for Shipping Media on commercial invoice
8. Report capability to know if the product price set up is done
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
8/8: Identified data source. For IP material bailed to IF and for external IF customers: it will be the IF CBA, per Jill.
7/14:  Closed RAID.  Craig will work ownership when issues arise offline.  
06/25: Documenting requirements completed & scenarios aligned, passed to Satya/Bharad for design. Lowered severity to P2.  Will extend to WW27 till Bharad confirms he has what he needs
6/20: Scenario Walk through and documenting requirements continuing; Need of CR to be assessed WW26.
6/16: Scenario Walk through and documenting requirements planned WW25.
6/12 (OM Team): The understanding is that the pricing would follow a cost/cost-plus model for the billings associated with STO, Please reach out to Praveen M from OTC pricing team if more inputs are needed 
 6/2: "Pricing on Commercial Invoicing on STO for WIP" being assessed for inclusion in FPR scope; pros/cons documentation WIP.  Next Checkpoint: WW25

Title: IP Returns boundary app R4C/MDG/GTS.
Description: In current state R4C (Salesforce) uses GTS to validate Return to Customer (RTC) and customer collection address via an API. RTC and collection address are not masteed in SAP MDG as these are Adhoc addresses that are captured during order creation.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
IAO GTS team doesn’t support non mastered addresses for GTS validation. Option 1 below is not possible, so Option 2 is currently the only remaining option. 

1.	Master data integrity – BP master data (name & address) must be stored in MDG.  This ensures data integrity and proper compliance validation in downstream systems, such as SAP GTS. RP: we discussed this during R4C design and dismissed this option – too many records would have to be created, customer does not have access to MDG so would not be able to enter the address, due to the approval flow creation of addresses would take too long. Also, R3 design closed last year – this process is not designed for in IAO
2.	Alterative approach – if integrating BP master data from MDG (option 1) is too complex, the process should follow a standard order flow.  In this approach, data is passed to GTS for compliance screening, which will either release or block the sales order based on official master data. RP: that’s the approach we agreed on during R4C design – GTS screening should happen at order level
Assigned to Name(s): Kimberly Moore, Kondapa Challa, Linda Y Nguyen
Decision Made: the process should follow a standard order flow.  In this approach, data is passed to GTS for compliance screening, which will either release or block the sales order based on official master data. RP: that’s the approach we agreed on during R4C design – GTS screening should happen at order level

Single solution approach for IF, where Intel manages warehousing.

Title: IP IF Pricing
Description: Pricing for Intel Products materials and manufacturing services is currently managed in spreadsheets that define pricing that require significant mapping to translate to future S/4 product nomenclature in a manner that can be used for Purchase Orders (from Intel Products to Intel Foundry) and Sales Orders (on Intel Foundry).  Decision is for how these data will be managed between SAP instances so that the correct pricing is ensured in the critical transaction documents so that internal accounting is robust and accurate.
In Progress Comments / Resolution: Ensure that pricing is maintained in the Central Finance instance such that every item and/or service that is procured by IP from IF has one and only one price for any time period. Pricing may be derived using multiple factors, or associated to multiple items, but the pricing table must have a single unique price in any given time period for a unique item or service.
Impacted Teams: FPR, FTS IP, OTC IF, PTP, Technology
Decision Made: Ensure that pricing is maintained in the Central Finance instance such that every item and/or service that is procured by IP from IF has one and only one price for any time period. Pricing may be derived using multiple factors, or associated to multiple items, but the pricing table must have a single unique price in any given time period for a unique item or service.

Title:
Need to confirm DMS tool solution. If it's Alfresco Content System (ACS), it requires funding for uplift.

https://intel.sharepoint.com/sites/Gotham/Shared%20Documents/General/Documentation/IAO%20Integration/IAO%20Issue%20Tracking.xlsx?web=1
Description:
Alfresco is currently a Tier 2 application for platform architecture, capacity and support model.  This was acknowledged over a year ago but due to CPM actions,  the initiative to up-level fell through the cracks. The resource manager and product owner have taken several AR’s address gaps. However, the app may not be Tier1 ready by R2 go-live

Issues:
•	There is only one ACMS non-prod system (called Benchmark).  All SAP non-prod (DevN, N+1, Mock 1/2/3/n, QA, Bench) point to that one ACMS non-prod system.  During periods of high UAT activity, the platform has become unresponsive.  See next bullet.
•	Alfresco platform availability – Adjustments have been made to increase the capacity of the platform (App containers on vm and DB on DBaaS).  ACMS platform team is also exploring if another non-prod environment is required.  They are also seeking funding to move from virtual to phsyical infrastructure.
•	There is a lot of noise being generated in SAP and sent to ACMS.  Workflow events coming from multiple SAP products are unecessarily storing xml files in ACMS.  This is related to the way SAP was integrated with ACMS (force all transactional or email attachments to store in the CMS).  Matt and Vijay are working with functional teams to stop generating these files.  This should significantly reduce the load.
•	Support model – platform is supported in Divya’s team.  I believe there are 1xAMR and 1xGAR engineers.  Application SME is just 1 person based in Costa Rica from Corporate Functions.  Product owner is seeking funding to hire additional resources.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
8/19 JohnC - Babu's direction is to proceed with next steps to roll back Alfresco for R3 as well since we don't have any performance fixes from the vendor. Next steps would be with the development and functional teams to work on alternate solution for document management for post-R3. Closing this RAID per Babu direction.

8/14 JohnC - Next checkpoint on decision for DMS solution is tomorrow 8/15. Got some answers from Hyland on Alfresco, but still waiting on answers from them with regard to how to improve platform performance.  Architect recommendation is to stay with Alfresco as long as all open issues have a mitigation/response. (see link to open issues)  Babu owns the final decision on that recommendation. JohnC to reach out to Babu on when he expects to make the call.

Title: E2E-94 Maintenance of Spare Parts
Description:
Slide #7:Overview of Maintenance process
1. MES/Workstream to trigger Maintenance order via XEUS/MARS.
2. SAP ME does not trigger, Maintenance notification (OOS) or Planned Maintenance order
3. Need not call the factory portal steps.
4. User status is not maintained during TECO.
5. No connection is required to MES/Workstream/SAP ME post TECO.
6. Repair details are not captured post Completion of Maintenance order.
Slide#8: Material availability & Material movement against the maintenance order
1. There is no STR process to pull material from 3PL plant.
2. New spare material is consumed from reservation or procure if there is no stock.
Slide#9: Expedite Process
1. There is no STR process to pull the material from GXO
Slide#14: Movement to EWM
1. Based on the reason code - repair. the parts are sent to IMR location in case of internal repair or to external via sub con process.
2. Existing repair process to be removed
Slide#15: Warranty claim
1. In case if the equipment is not under warranty it will follow the above repair process
2. Callout if the supplier is not on E2Open, a free goods PR is created with reference to the claim document
3. For the Non-MMID warranty process, the PR/PO generated is of type NB, whereas we had previously referred to it as ZEXP type
In Progress Comments / Resolution: Per detail design discussion workshop for PM and PTP team on maintenance process changes are made to E2E Process flow and same are updated to Signavio also.
Assigned to Name(s): Dave Mihir Jagdishchandra, Shankruth Shankruth Varma

Title: Single Operating Plan design GAP
Description:
Single operating plan is used for revenue planning, margin analysis, and customer supply commitments.
Bifurcated processes for Intel Foundry and external suppliers, the inability to refine the single operating plan, and the lack of sufficient data exchanges make it impossible to produce and operate to a single plan.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
11/14  'INT-CR01237- Multiple Sends downstream + External send downstream' approved to resolve 3), which closes this Risk.
11/7 1 and 2 below are OBS.  Tying out with Elizabeth White (working with Tom Sanger) regarding workaround and final solution.
9/23: Moving risk to R3 since the only pending stage (3) is for R3
6/30: plan to resolve this risk in 3 stages
1)	Manual process of synchronizing the erroneous signal for External products in OneMPS using the current UI that exists and copy and paste manual transactions.  We will have to do this at a minimum of 2 times per month taking roughly 1 person a day.  We may have to do it in ad hoc circumstances a couple addition times within the month.  While this process is adequate to go live, it does have some risk to data quality associated with the manual copy and paste activities, so we do not want to rely on this method for the entire 18 months between R2 and R3 – step2
-	No technical work required
-	Will document the business process and the mechanics to execute – Elizabeth White / Cameron (ww28)
-	Close on the supporting resource – Sanger/Tschider(ww32)

2)	Investigate support to reduce copy and paste risk.  We had a path outlined but it looks bigger, technically, than originally anticipated and with the current SPAN and OneMPS work pending we want to do some additional investigation before defining a clear path.  Potentially add a new schedule type for external or build macro support.   Need to weigh risk of error in copy and paste vs. workload in SPAN & OneMPS.  We have a couple paths to evaluate.  Potentially a hyper care solution path.  Spinnaker is assigning Cameron to work with Elizabeth on our manual process mechanics due ww28 to help us evaluate copy and paste risk & mitigation option.   We will revisit this step – ww29 Mcphaden/Ellis.  

3)	Incorporate editable constrained demand and publish capabilities into our R3 release.  These work around will not work for the complexity of our Back End build plans and S&OP processes.  It becomes simpler to manage since we will not have an integration with SvD or OneMPS boundary applications.
-	AR:  Spinnaker / Stutz / Carter
6/25: from PXT Tom/Dave will kickoff task force and explore Options and Alternatives considering R2 Go Live timeframe

Title: Getting Error in Standard API
Description:
1.	Case 1: API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV; v=2We are utilizing the API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV; v=2 for both "Get" and "POST" functions to S4.When calling the "Get" function, the API returns purchase contract details for standard Item Texts, but not for custom-created Item Texts such as K10. In these cases, the API provides only the key without the corresponding value. Similarly, when creating a Purchase Contract at the Item Level using the POST function, the item details are updated successfully. However, although the process indicates that the item text was created, the item text does not actually appear in the system.
2.	Case 2: api_purchaseorder_2 We are using api_purchaseorder_2 for both "Get" and "POST" functions to S4.When calling the "Get" function, the API returns purchase order details for standard Item Texts, but not for custom-created Item Texts. Again, only the key is provided, without the value.
3.	Case 3: API_INFORECORD_PROCESS_SRV Although the API schema includes a Material Group field, when using the GET function for API_INFORECORD_PROCESS_SRV, the Material Group is not populated, and its details are not returned. Could you please help us understand why these standard APIs are behaving this way and advise on possible resolutions for these issues? 
4.	Case 4: api_purchaseorder_2 and API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV (OData v2).We  During testing, we observed that the PATCH function is not working as expected for the Document Type "ZCAP". Notably, the PATCH operation works correctly for other document types, such as "ZMPO". Upon investigation and collaboration with the Deloitte Development Team, it was determined that the root cause is the absence of the 'NBF' field selection key in the T161 table for the Document Type "BSART = 'ZCAP'". However, our requirement involves using a custom document type and custom field selection copied from NBF and document type NB. There are differences in the field selection controls between these document types.
5.	Case 5: API_DMS_PROCESS_SRV (OData v2) has end Point Product Object Links which is not working as no Object link detail return during Get Function.
6.	Case6:  API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV (OData v2) for Document Management end point POST, DELETE and GET no Payload details are available on API hub. 
7.	How we can convert any Currency to USD as API will only provide which are available in Purchase document as standard, but we need to convert all in USD only.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
7/15: With IV Rao. They are working internally on this API. This was escalated to SAP.
7/23: [Richard T.] Removed my name as Assigned as have no knowledge, context or action on my behalf; have re-assigned back to person who raised this: to vivekyadav3@deloitte.com

Title: BPMG ERP/SCP Risk related to Business Processes standards' approved Change Request changes. The risk is that the BPM standard documentation would not be adequately documented after CRs are approved.
Description:
The risk is related to the Business Processes that went through changes that have been approved through the IAO Program Change Request Process. 
All BPM Standards (before Go-Live and  post-Go-Live) must be timely and accurately updated to reflect any approved CR changes. 

Existing IAO Program Change Request process does not adequately document steps needed to maintain accuracy of BPM standard documents (Signavio, BIC and SOPs) after a CR is approved. Additionally, an audit of R2 approved CRs is required to confirm Signavio and BIC flows have been updated to reflect the R2 change  requests.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
Documentation has been updated for R2 Hypercare process (through Brian O) and IAO Program Change Request process (through Alpna G and Anna T) to include expectations and steps for updating Signavio & Integrated IAO BPM standards for relevant approved changes.

R2 audits have also be done by the Towers to ensure approved R2 Change Requests have been incorporated into BPM standard documentation as relevant.

Title: Time Zone issue - ECA Reporting
Description:
We have noticed an issue with the ECA reports. It seems that the dates displayed are not in sequence  of the actual operation due to differences in time zones. From our initial findings, this discrepancy only impacts ECA reporting. We will also share this information with other tracks for their awareness.
The root cause of the issue is that SAP stamps UTC dates in some transaction tables and does not stamp system time (PST) in all transaction tables. SAP has informed us that this is their 'standard behavior'.
For Release 2, we have requested the ECA team to convert the transaction time zone to PST. We will continue to work with the Technical and SAP teams to find the right solution, ensuring that there is no impact on reporting.

Title: FSSD: Time Zone issue - ECA Reporting
Description: Standard SAP behavior is that it stamps UTC dates in table thereby providing a single, unambiguous point in time, regardless of where the data was created or where it's being accessed.  Issue seen in R2 UAT2 in an ECA reports where the dates displayed were not in sequence of the actual operation due to differences in time zones. All R2 Towers and Horizontals are requested to assess their UAT execution results for all reports and ensure the time stamp in reports is as expected.  Please complete this exercise by WW30.5 and mark this RAID as complete with your findings.  See detailed attached document for further details.  See RAID #1602 for additional details.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
08/06: FSSD issue has been resolved and the ECA code change done; Biz Testing also completed and good for Go live.  RAID is being closed
8/4: Resolution path with least impact assessed and on track for R2 Go Live: FSSD needs 2 fields Local and PST while ECA has only Local; ECA India team made the code changes on Mon 8/4 in Dev to enable PST stamp; and while CR is in WIP FSSD will complete code changes on Tues 8/5 and testing by Wed 8/6 morning pst. Need a Go decision from the business (Sunil Chebiyam) on Wed 8/6 morning; until then keep this P1
8/1: 15 FSSD reports are impacted.  Once ECA change is done, FSSD will need to make change across as some biz reports needed local time and some pacific. Risk of not making this change: customers will be sent data with incorrect timestamp. Next meeting WW32.Sun to find quickest and least impact solution.  Should not gate GLR.
7/31: FSSD needs more details from OTC, GLO, and inventory ECA teams, awaiting response. Checkpoint WW31.5.
7/25: CATTS saw & fixed issue back in June, iCOST reported no issues, FSSD is still checking and need confirmation on which time zone should be used and working with ECA, no other issues reported
Impacted Teams: Analytics (Reporting), B-Apps, FPR, FTS IF, Master Data, OTC IF
Assigned to Name(s): Ajith T, amy.shum@intel.com, Kelly Gerber, Lopa Chaudhry, Nitin Jain, Vanessa Clark

Title: Potential for BOM misalignment between PDH and IE's Transformation File
Description:
The Transformation file contains Bump & Sort Capacity Inputs provided by SCPs and IE's. The file will pull BOM data as of the Monday PRIOR to the following week's BY ESP Solve run  (I.e., WW1.1 Monday) 
However, PDH will pull the latest BOM data as of the Tuesday of the same week's BY ESP Solve run  (i.e., WW2.2 Tuesday, assuming WW2 = Current Week)
If changes are made between the prior Monday, and the current Tuesday, there will be multiple issues (i.e., Reticle, Capacity Group, Capacity Corridor Capacity planned to the wrong BOM/Rev Step)

We need to determine: 
A business process for the PDP/SPEED team to notify the IE's / SCP's if there are changes to the BOM, new rev steps, or new NPIs added
Options for IT to freeze the BOM

Title: FPR PC&IV: Intel Product  WIP Conversion
Description:
Risk: 
IP goes live "Big Bang" at R3. In order to properly value IP WIP and inventory the IP Production Orders need mapping to CIBR Lots. If the required data for lot to IP-PO linkage is not delivered as defined in this requirement, Intel will not be able to correctly assign cost to IP Production Orders at cut-over. This will result in incorrect unit costs for Intel Product, directly impacting financial accuracy.

Context:
As part of the WIP conversion process in R3 , we need to determine how to assign active lots, currently tracked in MES/Workstream but not yet in SAP, to their corresponding Intel Product (IP) Production Orders. This differs from the Intel Foundry (IF) side, where each IF lot has a 1:1 mapping to an IF Production Order (IF PO), allowing direct unit upload and costing using lot-level data from ICOST.

Scope of This Requirement:
This requirement focuses on the Intel Product (IP) side, where there is no 1:1 relationship between IP Production Orders (IP POs) and lots. It is necessary to determine which IF lots should be assigned to fulfill those orders, enabling proper PO creation and downstream costing.

Finance Requirements for Costing:
To enable costing for IP, Finance needs Planning to provide the following information:

•	IP Production Order reference, including the associated IP UPI, location, and quantity
•	CIBR Lots assigned to each IP Production Order, with full details such as IF UPI, exact lot number, location, and quantity

Title: FPR: Inventory Trend Model for financial forecasting data -  OneMPS Solve EOL (related to RAIDs 1860 & 1963).
Description:
Risk: 
FPR Inventory Trend Model would not be able to produce bi-weekly short term financial forecasting data. The trend model data is a part of essential CFO's office data package. If not fixed, the risk is to have significantly inaccurate amounts of forecasted reserves up to $200M per quarter. The impact is expected starting WW41.

Requirements: 
a)   Raw Die Consumption
b) Is Wafer starts & Is TPT
c) Test/Assy Yields & UPWS
d) Sort to Die Prep Mapping, including Test out and Sort out.

Details: 
FPR relies on SDA reports sourced from OneMPS Solve (PSC5 Report, Wafer Recon report, UPWS report, Sort to Die Prep Mapping & DLCP Percentages report, & Cube AIR Report for Test & Sort Outs) to run its short term inventory forecasting. The issue and risk are caused by OneMPS solve EOL, including the PSC5 report EOL at R2 go-live impacting the report users. Additionally, EOL of Sort Outs with R2 go live with Cube AIR. A new connection and report need to be made to run the same financial forecasting process within the Trend Model. A new connection and report needs to be made to run the same forecasting process. Sort-out Data is needed for Inventory Trend Model in a consumable format to run short term financial forecasting.

10/13 -  3/6 reports delivered. 1 Report still having issues and working with the developer to get it resolved.

Title: SCP development velocity

Critical Path & Triage: Immediately identify and escalate the most business-critical and high-risk objects in the backlog. 
Frequent Progress Monitoring: Move to more frequent mini stand-ups or checkpoints to track completion of business-critical and high-risk objects and unblock issues in real time.
Resource Augmentation / Swarming: Temporarily reassign or "swarm" additional resources from completed/non-critical areas to accelerate bottleneck items.

Title: Credit Collections Dashboard
Description:
We have proactively addressed several critical roadblocks in the UI ( User Interface) for the Credit and Collections Dashboard, 

1.	Technology Alignment:
Upon review, we determined that the initial technology choice for the dashboard did not align with our requirements. The team has now pivoted to the traditional FIORI approach, which provides the flexibility we need to deliver on expectations.

2.	Filter Logic Improvement:
During the recent FIORI platform demonstration to C&C, we discovered a gap in the filter logic that necessitated backend rework. The team has promptly incorporated these enhancements into the updated functional specification.

Risks:
It is important to recognize that any new change request—regardless of size—could jeopardize delivery timelines and introduce further downstream impacts. To maintain momentum and minimize disruption, we recommend all proposed changes be formally documented and reviewed after January 16th, at which point we can determine next steps.
In Progress Comments / Resolution: 11/21/2025: Since identifying these issues, Akshay and the team have partnered closely with our technology group to launch the next version of the C&C dashboard. Revised delivery timelines have already been communicated as follows: IP/IF Dashboard FUT Conduct - 12/24/25, IP/IF Dashboard FUT Review and Approve - 01/16/26 (accounting for holidays)

Title: Cross program test coordination alignment
Description: Appears to be an ongoing GAP in who owns all test coordination.  Background:  string test scope for each tower managed individually with string tests crossing towers.  E2E team is driving specific test scenarios/cases for end to end.  External supplier requires supplier onboarding test cases.  Data for all the scenarios needs to be identified and created to NOT overlap and impact test cases of other teams.   GAP ->  Towers think E2E team is coordinating this.  E2E is saying it isn't doing the end to end scenario then towers should coordinate themselves.  So no one owns making sure everyone is aligned, correct test data is setup for each teams test cases synchronized from DP>SP>PTP for PR/PO creation, and how do these all run in parallel.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
12/18 : Confirmed with PTP Test Lead and Jose and now this can be closed . Hence closing this RAID                                                                                                        12/12 Russ to connect with B2B and E2O team to align on best approach to collect/map data for supplier testing. Recommend one consolidated sheet with all test cases/scenarios with data mapped against each ETA to review in PTP workshop 12/18

12/10 Agreed to escalate the risk level to P1 for external partner testing due to ongoing unresolved questions, and Viswa committed to summarizing the open issues and seeking input from test team and E2E.   Original closure date pushed from 12/5 to 12/19.

Open questions:
PTP/Cross Tower:
- If a PTP test case is covered by E2E, do we still need to run the PTP test case or is it closed?  
- If cross tower, are we manually creating the start of a string test if the process starts in another tower OR will that be triggered by process flow?  ex:  PR creation by SCP to inital procurement flow
- How to not have data overlap?  Cannot test the same material, POs, etc with multiple test cases at the same time.
- IP/IF  STO ownership for E2E coordination?

Supplier specific:
- For each test cases, are we simulating supplier or actually using pilot?

Title: ERPM business partners will be created as organizations under customer partner type “02” in GTS
Description: Currently, business partners from ERPM are created in GTS as contact persons (partner type “3”), which prevents their use as customers in IRC transactions. This change ensures more standardized process and better data consistency moving forward.

Title: Issue with Immediate Processing and Manual Intervention Required for HRMM IDoc Posting
Description:
Immediate Processing:
When using Immediate Processing, the data does not load, and the process is cancelled with status 64. After trying to reprocess using BD87, the status changes to 52 and the message is “Application Document is not posted.”
Example IDoc: 1752174

Background Processing:
When loading IDocs via background jobs (status 64), reprocessing with BD87 does post the records, but three notifications appear for each IDoc. If I do not manually click the ‘Back’ button after each notification, the next IDoc does not process and the screen remains stuck.
Example IDoc: 1752394

We have over 200,000 HRMM records, and clicking ‘Back’ for every single IDoc is not practical.

Title: Legacy middleware for boundary app straddle testing not setup.  Issues for many apps trying to connect to both FC0 and FM1
Description:
By straddle area, need to determine whether SAP ECC FM1 (Mock) or FC0 (QA) will be used for straddle testing.
Reason to use FM1:  Master data and transactional data will match, ECA is connected to FM1.  Some apps will have to do manual work to make the data match.
Reason to use FC0:  Cost and effort is much less.  Middleware not currently setup to FM1 which will be a significant amount of work.  Some apps (Readsoft) do not have licenses for FM1.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
2/17/26:  No commercial impact, Deloitte will do PI/PO development.  All interfaces identified and in progress.  Closing RAID.  
2/3/26:  Deloitte will be doing the PI/PO development.  6 applications identified needing PI/PO interfaces.  144 PI/PO Interfaces have been submitted.  Need a new SOW and Chris Woodburn will request budget and PO Approval.  
12/17: Decision was made by Core Leadership to enable SAP PI/PO interfaces to new ECC Mock environments from all boundary apps involved in R3-R5 straddle testing.  We will also enable an EDW Mock Schema for testing.  All R3-R5 Straddle testing should use the SAP ECC FM1/FM2 environments, not the FC0 environment (unless you received a specific exception approval).  FM1/FM2 will be used for ITC1,2,3 and UAT1.  FB0 (Bench) will be used for UAT2 and FC0 (QA) will be used for UAT3. This decision will ensure a stable and consistent Path to Production during 2026 for new deals and bug fixes, while also ensuring a production-like environment for IAO testing. Additional funding will be provided for CW support for the SAP PI/PO development and EDW Ab Initio development.  Boundary app teams are working on providing a list of interfaces to enable. This RAID will be reduced from P0 to P2.  It will be closed only when we have begun execution on all development.
Impacted Teams: B-Apps, Cutover, FTS IF, FTS IP, Infrastructure, Master Data, PTP, Technology, Test Management

Title: Company Code address disappearing in DI0 250
Description:

Title: Need SMH Application TLS 1.2 stablishing and testing for JDBC connection to MARS
Description:
Intel SMH application Security requirements:  PDF team must confirm that the application architecture meets all security requirements.  The pending item for TLS 1.2 is connection to MARS 
MARS's Oracle Listener only supports RSA Key Exchange, many older Oracle Database versions (and some default listener configurations) only advertise cipher suites like: TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA, etc. Mars's Oracle version supports TLS 1.2 but only with RSA Key exchange suites. This is for all MARS databases as was told by their DBA Deepan. Behnam has provided a test Java code for POC to programmatically inject the RSA cipher suite. Mars connectivity with three months IAO delay is now needed by end of March 2026.

Title: SMG: Managed Services transition to Accenture. Overall risk is about Accenture ramping up on SMG application and becoming knowledgeable enough to support SMG boundary application from weighing on process changes as well as test signoff
Description: SMG SMEs are going to go away and Accenture is taking their place. The risk is loss of tribal knowledge and Accenture not knowledgeable enough to weigh in on critical matters such as process changes and test scenario signoffs
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
04/02 - Things are pretty much the same. Potential risk exists. We'll wait until ITC starts and then look into closing the risk
03/03 - Things haven't stabilized with SMG ops transition sill in Amber status. The risk still remains if any significant biz process impact analysis is required, Accenture might stumble. I'm keeping risk open for another month. But we're active trying to stay on top of it
02/02: No change. Accenture is now in charge of biz operations in most areas. There are some hits and misses due to lack of expertise. Let's monitor things until end of the quarter before we close this RAID
01/05: This is a placeholder risk. SMG has almost completed the transition of responsibilities from Intel BB's to Accenture for biz operation functions. We need to monitor how SMG responds to any biz process changes or TCM needs for next few weeks/months before we can close this RAID item. Extending the due date by a month
12/01: We’ll really start to see the impact of this risk now as SMG BB folks have started to leave Intel at the end of November and some will be leaving at the end of December. So, we don’t have SMEs to rely on going forward. Any biz process open, any test scenario approval, we’ll have to depend upon Accenture going forward. The nature of the risk is somewhat unique as we don't know exactly what could turn out to be a roadblock but there is a potential. Title: FPR Origami BApp build delayed - Dependency on Tririga app
Description:
Build delayed due to dependency on Tririga app (Bapps in Mfg Integration Misco)

Dependency sequence: Tririga IAPM# (Bapps in Mfg Integration Misco – CDI Arch: Singh, Pradipkumar) → ECA → Origami
•	Tririga build completes - WW8.1 (2/16)
•	ECA views rebuild complete – WW10.1 (3/2)
•	Overall Origami build completion - WW10.2 (3/3)

Data issue and design/FUT testing gaps. The delay is attributed to Tririga, as the building related columns required by ECA are not yet available in the Corporate Master Data zone. These columns currently exist only within Tririga and are accessible solely in the IP zone. The solution requires consuming data from the Corporate Master Data zone together with ECA data, but cross sharing from the IP zone to the Corporate zone is not permitted under the approved architecture. Therefore, Tririga must expose the necessary building related information to the Corporate zone before ECA can proceed.

Title: Review process for post-validation of system changes
Description: When platform team makes a change/fix to SMH system/environment, the process needs to ensure recovery is truly completed.  Suggestion is to review Post-Change Functional Validation (PCFV) procedure and look at context appropriate smoke tests to verify.  Specific example is the issue created recently (Feb 4, 2026) when an effort was made to look at the build.sh file that SMH uses to build orchestrations (and set via crontab) and look for lines that echo the github password., then comment them out.  However, one of those echo statements was inside a bash function and was necessary for it to function.  After the work was done and SMH was seemingly up and operational, projects could not build.  The functional testing team lost testing time over night, having to wait until next morning to correct the issue.  A more rigorous PCFV could have drawn out the issue sooner.

As per the initial FS walkthrough, we had understood the requirement to be posting 3 documents in the first phase, and accordingly we had started the development. Title: Delay in PTPXR103 - Blocked and QI Stock Report to be completed in S17

Team was working on building the report PTPXR103 - Blocked and QI Stock Report for S17. However, ECA team discovered mid-sprint that the logic provided in the FSD did not meet the reporting requirements. This resulted in having to make changes in the back-end (CF and CC view) before we can apply those changes to the report (front end).

This report will be pushed to be completed in S18 due to having to provided the updated mapping logic in the view in S18 and hopefully the report can also be done S18.

10/28: Meeting scheduled tomorrow to continue discussion - summary is below:
•	The report request originated from PTP, and the requirement is housed under PTP.
•	However, the data source is FTS. Since this requirement was not from FTS, they are not owning the TUT approval.
•	The roadblock is that, since Deloitte designed the report (TDD), neither PTP nor FTS is able to provide TUT approval for the report design itself.
o	However, Deloitte only designed the report because we were not getting any traction on the TDD, which is why the team started the build based on the available details provided to us – FSD, and Views. 
o	We also connected with Tower + FTS Intel team to get an understanding of the relationship in the semantic layer.
•	Both PTP and FTS have confirmed they can validate and offer conditional approval for their respective data-related attributes.
•	Deloitte does not have the authority to provide the TUT approval on the design.

Title: Delays in Resolving UOM Discrepancies in Rinchem (Wiings) Impacting R3 B2B Development Timeline
Description:
We have observed that UOMs used by some Suppliers & 3PLs are not available in SAP out-of-the-box nor are they part of an international standard such as UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20.  This raises the overall concern about either Intel or our Suppliers using custom codes for UoM, Country of Origin, and other codes used in international trade.  

We need to answer the questions of:
1) What are the negative consequences of having non-standard UoMs (Ex: customs blockage)
2) If the consequences are significant, should Intel commit itself to aligning to a global standard such as UN/CEFACT?

This misalignment poses a risk to the development process and urgently requires a decision.

Some possible outcomes are:
A) We commit to only use SAP S4 UoM codes and force Suppliers/3PLs to select from a list of codes available in S4 (Ex: The custom UoM of "NowPak" becomes the SAP "Container").  These UoMs may not align with international standards and this impacts Suppliers.
B) We align to a global standard such as UN/CEFACT.  This impacts Suppliers but will be easier for customs/government agencies to interpret.
C) We add custom UoM codes to SAP.  This minimizes the impact on Suppliers.

While we've observed this misalignment while working with RinChem, RinChem is NOT the problem.  The misalignment with RinChem is a symptom of the broader problem.  The inability to finalize these UOMs is delaying the start of development work with Rinchem, a Chem 3PL to be integrated via OES4. This delay threatens to impact the overall timeline for R3, particularly the FUT phase, which is crucial for testing and validation.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:

Title: Spares (WIINGS) Inventory conversion strategy and process to valuate items post go-live purchases
Description: Solution clarification is needed for 1. How we load the inventory that is already expensed in S/4 HANA 2. How the item inventory will be evaluated for any new purchases thereafter
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
09/18: PtP team will activate the SAP Split variation explained in the email attached in this Rad item. Raid can be closed.
Shared the design details to Intel FPR, PTP team. Awaiting for the final confirmation from Intel team
08/26:Costing alignment completed
Material Master : Split valuation will be activated for Spare parts with valuation types New, Damaged, Repair/refurbished, Harvest and Converted (for existing inventory conversion).​
Costing type: Use moving average price for all spare parts valuation types (New, Damaged, Repair/refurbished, Harvest and Converted) with initial cost as $0 or $0.01.​
Moving average Price at MM ID: First purchase order (Standard PO with New part or Sub-con PO with Repair part) Good receipt/Invoice receipt amount will be updated as moving average price at MMID and it will be revised based on the subsequent GR/IR’s.​
Inventory Migration: Existing spare parts inventory (ECC stock) would be migrated to S4 with valuation type converted and inventory value would be $0 or $0.01.​
Inventory consumption at Plant Maintenance (PM) order: PM order must look for conversion stock first. If not available, then consume new parts​

Open item: Some spare parts are not consolidated (same part will be used as damaged or Repair). From costing side, maintaining the dual cost for the same part without valuation type is not possible. PTP/Logistics team need to discuss and finalize the to be approach

In Progress Comments / Resolution:
10/30 Documenting KDD in RAID - open items below
10/29 Jorge Sanchez email 10/29 transposed: ...assume this ‘manual process’ needs to be added to the ‘change impact assessments’ , to manage accordingly, 
10/28: Krishna email 10/28 transposed:
Analyzed the data pulled by GLO IT team, and the CIBR team:
Number of Test to Finish(FG01) (Both Internal and External) movement data from SAP FP0 and CIBR EDW ~342K transactions
(data from Ramesh M)
Number of 913 movements (MB51) over the last 6 months – approx. 8000.  Extend to 1 year, approx. 16k transactions.
(Data from Sundari Devi, GLO IT)
Martin’s analysis:
I tried to estimate the number of rework orders for this 6 month of data with ~8000 individual lots at 300 individual FG MM id (rework order for OSAT per MMID)
•	493 combinations of MMID and month - assuming one rework order per month and MMID - 20 rework orders per week
•	856 combinations of MMID and day - assuming one rework order per day and MMID - 30 rework order per week
We initially assumed 10 mins per transaction (per lot) – this turns out is wildly overstated in terms of effort since in SAP you can copy paste many lines in one shot before updating.
Guidance from Syed, Murthy was to use more closer to 1 min per lot.
16k lot transactions @ 1 min roughly equates to roughly 5 hours a week of manual transactions.
While the volumes aren’t insignificant, I doubt just savings would pass through the CR process.
Hence I recommend that we cancel this CR from going into the CR ingestion.
Open items (create a RAID to track it)
-	What report will provide the report of rework lots that require GR transaction
-	Will IP inventory controls person be able to run this report on their own, or need someone in IF to send
-	Develop the business process, R&R for how the manual process would work.
11/20: Will create new RAID for these asks

A new “timezone” field will be provided so downstream consumers can convert the timestamp into the timezone they need
The change has wide downstream impact, including Fab POLP, and is creating a schedule risk due to required downstream updates and re-validation.

Title: R3 Returns Rework Process Causing Finance Double Counting in ICOST
Description:
During the review to use the R3 Returns Rework process for Anafi product retest, we determined that the current process did not fully account for Finance impacts and will result in double counting in the iCost tool.
To address this, we need to:
Redesign the R3 Returns Rework process to eliminate double counting in iCost
Incorporate requirements and impacts for the FTS, FPR, P2P, and OTC teams
Align stakeholders on the new process and validate financial accuracy before deployment
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
3/23: Volume of transactions expected to be very small (<1 month); Finance will handle with journal entries until R3 Go Live. Need to assess what the volume will be through R5 (could change when we add IPG, need to obtain IPG history that we can reference).  If the volume continues to be small and can be handled with journal entries, we should avoid a complex change if it is not justified by the labor savings.  Post R5, since iCOST goes away, this ceases to be an issue.
3/13 Joey: ICOST has a plan for workaround for R2 for Altera for the period across 13 weeks (1 MM).
3/3: E2E to sync WW11 when Todd is back
Impacted Teams: E2E, FPR, FTS IF, OTC IF, PTP
Assigned to Name(s): Aishwarya Bhat, eugene.celis@intel.com, Kalpesh K Shah, talbot.smith@intel.com

Title: Gaps identified with ICOST data feeds into a HANA for Single Source Of Truth (SSOT) views
Description: ICOST is only one of the data feeds into a HANA view called SSOT – single source of truth.  This particular view pulls from Revenue and ICOST, and is used for forecast.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
17/Mar - Team further reviewed design options and determined that changes to existing HANA views are not the best option, so looking at either iCost->HANA->ECA->SAC OR  iCost->ECA->SAC, planning to close by 27/Mar

12/Mar - Draft high level design complete, reviewing with the biz owners today and will update accordingly. Plan to go DARB for approval WW12 then HANA TRG.  Will look at creating CR.
5/Mar - Biz (Mandera, Rina - Business Owner (IF) & Gilchrist, Robert D - Business Owner (IP))  plans to complete FSD by WW13.4 and Jeet/Srini will completed high-level data spec by WW13.5, then present to the HANA team WW14.
We will give them a new view in HANA instead of updating the old one to prevent any impact to other DSAs.  We don’t want to do this in ECA yet because of legacy data unless we want to do a straddle design in ECA. It will be more effort. Eventually everything will move to SAC, S4 CFIN and I believe there is no separate BW system going forward so the BPC module is embedded in SAP S4 & S4 CFIN.

SAC team already has the list of HANA views that are in question, but we do not know which ones need to be changed yet.  The problem statement is that the existing HANA views use iCOST data at a particular SNOP product hierarchy level. But, for R3, the data needs to be at a different product hierarchy level (specifically at MMID).  Team is going to clarify requirements and impacts then consider the options:
1.	Is it better to modify or create new HANA views by bringing in new sources (iCOST data is already available in HANA)? We may have to bring in new sources into HANA.
2.	Ingest iCOST data into ECA assuming other source data is already available in ECA and build the views in ECA. But this would be a throw away solution because post R5 iCOST is going away.
Impacted Teams: B-Apps, Data Foundation Program (ECA) & Embedded Data Support
Assigned To (Team): Data Foundation Program (ECA) & Embedded Data Support

Title: R2 IP SP Convergence Analysis between COMPASS and BY
Description:
R2 design puts BY IBP (wafer planning) in the middle of 2 existing applications OneMPS/COMPASS. Systems will not match but require a business reasonableness. Having reasonable business data flows across these 3 apps is what we call convergence. 
BY IBP/COMPASS – convergence not completed:
Tested 10 types of IP products (list matches original OneMPS convergence list)
70% of the products have converged well.  30% have opens
E2E testing of COMPASS to SvD has not been done
The IP team thinks the divergence issues reside in aligning parameters, plus BOH alignment of BY/OneMPS but still needs to be demonstrated and this can’t be done before Go-Live
Go-Live Impact: 
IP has a month after go-live to sort out disconnects before we would need to make judgements to operational decisions (ww46-48 timeframe).
Two judgement types: 
S&OP/SvD would need to use BY input instead of Compass for issues like projecting reserves
Materials/capacity decisions for ATM.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
WW48: Nov Compass Convergence summary:
- >75% products converged well (within 10%) 
- Focus on high volume product divergence and deep divve
- Compass vs BY divergence driven primarily by incoming OneMPS BOH divergence, BOM setup issues in BY, paremeter mismatches, end of horizon aligment
Team continues evalueting remaining gaps and requires hte parameter matchinf reporting and dev rules from Spinnaker

WW37: Below waiver approved during UAT2 Exit
Between ww38-ww42, cross functional team* to: 
Map BY to Compass product groupings
Align parameters between BY-Compass per groupings
Do the weeks of Inventory alignment between compass, BY, and OneMPS
Develop the reports required for Compass (like ones created for OneMPS)
E2E test with SvD

Between ww43-ww46
Evaluate fixes above for convergence improvement
Evaluate any convergence gaps remaining for impact and choices going forward

WW47 implement BOH fix for OneMPS to BY alignment

(*) The above plan will require a dedicated team

Title: FPR: ICOST Straddle: Breakpoint from  ICOST/CIBR Straddle WtF: inventory transitions from mfg to FG
Description:
CIBR/Xeus inventory transitions from mfg to FG.  	• The concern is about accurately recognizing and mapping the transition of inventory from manufacturing to finished goods in the new S4 process, specifically ensuring that only the correct goods receipt (101 movement) is picked up as finished goods, rather than all intermediate movements. 1
	• This is critical because the process is changing from a write-on movement type to a goods receipt off a production order, which may impact how ICOST and CIBR recognize finished goods inventory, especially during the straddle period when not all sites are live on S4. 2
	• The group agreed this is a significant break point that requires careful attention to avoid errors in inventory recognition and downstream processing.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
