Title:
Intel support needed: View/report filter conflict. Awaiting confirmation.
PTPFPR057	GPR-Paid Report
PTPXR113	GPR-Paid Details Report
Description:
we identified that the current filter condition is preventing any results from being fetched.
Filter:
•	finance_s4.accounting_document_header_line:
•	ledger_account_type_cd = 'K'
•	AND accounting_document_nbr != LEFT(intercompany_posting_nbr, 10)
•	finance_s4_if.universal_journal_entry_line_items ujli
•	ujli.supplier_party_id NOT LIKE 'IV%' 
•	AND ujli.ledger_account_type_cd = 'K'
•	AND ujli.accounting_ledger_cd = '0L' 
Join Condition:
LEFT(adhl.intercompany_posting_nbr, 10) = ujeli.accounting_document_nbr
AND ujeli.company_cd = SUBSTR(adhl.intercompany_posting_nbr, 11, 3)
AND ujeli.fiscal_year_nbr = adhl.fiscal_year_nbr
We identified that the current filter condition—specifically the use of Ledger Account Type Code = K in the ACDOCA table—is preventing any results from being fetched.
However, upon removing this filter, we were able to retrieve the expected data. Could you please revisit this part of the code and assess whether the filter is necessary?
Let us know if you'd like to connect with the EP team to discuss this further.

Decision Made: From an end-to-end process perspective, virtual shipment currently has no impact to be called out, as the shipped inventory is not being returned to SAP as re-bailed stock —a requirement expected to be covered in the future R3 state. Once these documents are ready, we will schedule a review with Intel counterparts to ensure all required details are accurately captured and updated.

Title: R3 RAR Group Valuation Posting for Cost
Description:
Intel utilizes multiple valuation approaches in the SAP Material Ledger to manage inventory costs, capturing both legal (local) and group (consolidated) valuations. For group valuation, currency type 31 is used in Financial Accounting (FI) to record inventory at group prices. However, SAP Revenue Accounting & Reporting (RAR) does not support currency type 31 or any third local currency.
As a result, RAR cannot access or process group valuation values. This leads to a discrepancy (GAP): inventory and cost postings in FI, especially for transactions such as Post Goods Issue (PGI)—reflect both legal and group valuations, while RAR postings are limited to legal valuation only. This limitation means RAR-based cost recognitions and FI postings are not fully aligned, potentially impacting inventory and margin reporting, particularly for group-level financial analyses.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
10/22: - Pramod: We collaborated with the technical team to develop a proof of concept (POC) focused on reading group valuation amounts and analyzing postings at the ACDAOC table level.
The POC was reviewed with the Intel revenue business and leadership teams and has received approval. We are currently in the process of creating a change request (CR), pending confirmation of technical efforts from the technical team.

10/10 - We are collaborating internally with the technology team to develop a proof of concept aimed at reading group valuation amounts and analyzing their posting at the ACDOCA table level. This initiative is focused on exploring technical solutions to accurately capture and reflect group valuation data in financial postings, helping to address current system limitations and align inventory costing across relevant processes.
09/30 - In parallel, we are collaborating with the SAP Product Team, who are actively analyzing the requirement to enable Revenue Accounting & Reporting (RAR) to read group valuation data. The goal is to ensure RAR can correctly post group valuation amounts to the ACDOCA table, specifically utilizing the Offsetting Account for Ledger (OSL) field. This enhancement would help bridge the current gap between inventory/cost postings in FI and those reflected in RAR, supporting more accurate group-level financial reporting
Latest Comments:
10/16 - Currently under review with SAP.
10/10 - We are collaborating internally with the technology team to develop a proof of concept aimed at reading group valuation amounts and analyzing their posting at the ACDOCA table level. This initiative is focused on exploring technical solutions to accurately capture and reflect group valuation data in financial postings, helping to address current system limitations and align inventory costing across relevant processes.

Title: LOGE1535 - Missing critcal logic in the FS
Description: In the Functional Specification (FS) , Disposition Confirmation scenario requires additional attention regarding process steps: This scenario is challenging, as it initiates all subsequent actions. The functional team is currently uncertain which BAPI should be used to split the inspection document. Identifying the appropriate BAPI will require additional investigation. This scenario also involves several detailed processing steps.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
11/06: Solution suggested by SAP, didn't work as expected. Team has replied back to SAP with the same result and awaiting further resolution approach. As discussed and aligned in the meeting today, assigning back the defect to Tech.
11/03 : We are currently working on a workaround with SAP. Although we implemented the change recommended by Stanley from the SAP team, the issue still exist. 
OSS message # 1275146/2025 to SAP. And also we have scheduled a call with Stanley tomorrow to further investigate.
10/16: Step by Step Inspection Split process screenshots provided to Tech team to investigate further on the BAPI FM.
10/17: TECH TEAM UPDATE: We have researched the functionality we want to replicate from the /SCWM/QINSP transaction (TCode), specifically using BAPI, function modules, or APIs. Unfortunately, there is no standard object available that allows us to automate this functionality, which users currently execute manually through the transaction. My investigation included a review of SAP documentation for EWM processes, but there are no references to an API or BAPI for this purpose. We checked with our SME's, and they confirm the same limitation. Additionally, after debugging, I found that the process consists of several steps that cannot be easily replicated with straightforward ABAP logic.
Based on these findings, I suggest two possible options:
Raise a ticket with SAP: We can submit a request describing the process we want to replicate within a custom program and wait for their official guidance or solution.
Use CALL TRANSACTION: By executing TCode /SCWM/QINSP within a custom program, we can attempt to replicate the required functionality. However, creating a proof of concept will be essential, especially considering that splitting may involve many item lines (potentially more than 1,000). We can use transaction SHDB to record the actions required for /SCWM/QINSP. For testing purposes, we will need new test data with at least 10 split scenarios.
11/06: OSS notes created with SAP for way forward. Clisng this raid.

This is a design decision to be taken by functional team in consent with SAP team

Description: If a SFG material is not ready to ship from origin site or not ready to be received at destination site, factory must hold the material.

Intel Foundry SFG docks are shipping docks, not warehouses; there is no capacity for storage.

Title: Fab Traceability
Description: Define business rules for COO of primary die for WLA or multi-die products.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
12/4  Final piece in place, and closing RAID per following:
The design / process will essentially match our current state (Pre-IAO)
-	When a packaged unit has multiple pieces of IP Silicon, the piece of silicon that ties to the function of the product being built will be used to identify country of fabrication.  I believe they refer to this as the Functional Die.
    o	If it is a Graphis product then the Graphics silicon will be used.  
    o	If it is a CPU then the CPU silicon will be used
-	When a Lot/Batch contains a mixture of units from more than one Country of Fabrication
    o	Logistics & Warehouse operations have an existing design in place that references the ULT (Unit Level Traceability) system to retrieve the necessary information.
    o	This enables the Labels and Commercial Invoices to be populated properly
    o	This theoretically enables Warehouse Operations to do Batch/Box splitting and/or pick & pack specific units based on their Country of Fabrication (if necessary).  Not sure how automated or manual this is.
-	Population of the IP S/4 Batch Characteristic for Country of Diffusion (Fab)
    o	If a Lot/Batch contains units that were fabricated in only one country, then that particular country code is populated.
    o	If a Lot/Batch contains a mixture of units from more than one Country of Fabrication
          	The value “FVR” is populated in the Country of Diffusion (Fab) batch characteristic
          	The value “FVR” triggers systems/users to go reference the ULT system to retrieve the necessary information

Title: Partial Holds Process
Description: Meeting to determine how to handle and communicate holds and partial holds triggering text POs / Build instructions, such as splitting orders and updating statuses, and to ensure the process is documented and aligned with Eugene and Isaac.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
10/17  Met with Martin and Hernani.  Summary of call:  The meeting focused on resolving the process complexities related to external OSATs (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test services), specifically addressing the definition and handling of holds and partial holds within production orders. Key points discussed include how partial holds require splitting production orders into child orders, the necessity to immediately communicate these splits and holds to external OSATs, and the operational challenges related to splitting lots and merges both in assembly and final test phases. There was consensus that the child orders on hold must be communicated without delay to external OSATs (using standard message codes such as 3A4). The discussion also clarified that split lots do not automatically split production orders unless Intel explicitly decides so and that lot splits within assembly must be handled carefully. The group acknowledged existing tools like PDF SMH for monitoring lot splits at final test, and identified scenarios where manual intervention is still required, especially for merges involving less-than-full box quantities. Finally, the team highlighted discrepancies between processes used by OSATs versus ODMs, noting that in ODM contexts buyers typically handle order modifications, whereas planners handle production orders for OSATs.   -  CLOSED
10/16  Shilpa requested inclusion of this topic in Haritha Komma's IF/IP Task Force call.  Shilpa raised the issue and answer is that the child order needs to be sent to IF.  NEXT STEPS:  Meet with Martin and Hernani to discuss (adding to RAID 02670 call topics).

I collaborated with Stacey to define the required columns for this report.

Current Status: Awaiting team's feasibility assessment from GTT analytics team

Title: CAAS API1 Steet 5 inclusion in Smart Split logic
Description: Following the current smart split logic for StreetTxt field in API1, the street address is divided across five different fields Street 1 (60 characters) and Street 2–5 (40 characters each) in GTS, giving a maximum capacity of 220 characters.  However, we have identified that Street5 (Location) is not being considered in SPL screening as part of standard SAP.  Analysis of Webquote, ERPM, Reltio, and Profisee - see a combined <1000 examples -  no examples of legitimate street addresses more than 180 characters. Instead, these are cases of poor data quality where they fit the entire address into the street field, rather than only the street field. Or they include instructions.
In Progress Comments / Resolution:
2/3 - Per alignment with Deloitte and IAO trade team - decision to use the smart-split logic to still store in location(Street5) but do not run SPL on street5 as we know that is not true address. This is to avoid impact and not to have SPL impact as well due to unwanted street checks. In future if required, enhancement can be done to include Street5 in SPL.
Key word:  IAOTrade
Assigned to Name(s): ERICKA FERGUSON, JEAN-MARC LENC, piyushx.ashok.patil@intel.com, R Prem Siddharth

Title: Commercial Value Gap for Anafi Customs Clearance
Description:
There is a gap in sending the value of products at the different Anafi invoice points, which customs officials require to clear customs. The value must include all components and services used to create the invoiced material. CBAs cannot be expected to calculate the commercial values. The correct commercial value must be sent to the CBAs with the orderable part number and the validity date, and they will upload it into the S/4 system.

If we do not clarify this, it will impact data migration in WW08 and go-live.

For STOs, the value will come from Prospal until R3. An automated solution is being researched as part of a separate RAID (03112) for R3 to address those concerns; however, this will not be completed in time for Anafi go-live.

Title: Delay in Manufacturing Execution Design Impacts Training Deliverables for Intel Foundry Forecast to Stock Manufacturing Execution
Description: The design of the Manufacturing Execution process for the Intel Foundry Forecast to Stock Manufacturing Execution project has not been completed on schedule and is now expected to be finished in work week 17. This delay is putting downstream project deliverables at risk, including change impact analysis, audience analysis, persona alignment, and curriculum review/planning. As a result, these milestones may not be completed on time.

This could prevent readiness for user acceptance testing (UAT) and reduce the team’s ability to support end users effectively. The delay may also affect project timeline adherence and overall implementation readiness.
Assigned to Name(s): arjun.puri@intel.com, Kelly Gerber, Scott Yeager, wendy steiner

Title: BY - OTC IF : Replace virtual plant on SO with actual plant
Description:
Plant Determination on SO  (for everything apart from IP-IF order creation via BI)
1. Understanding during  R3 design :Plant determination on the SO's in R3 will be based on MDG logic to add virtual plant on the MMID which inturn gets updated on the SO based on the feed from BY as an actual plant during the order confirmation. If MMID in S4 already has actual plant then BY does not update the plant on the SO
2. Problem statement : BY team has mentioned that their current design does not send the plant information to S4 and if we could explore the customer sending the plant info via EDI to update on the order or alternatively determine actual plant based on MMID ( similar to R2 design which has it's limitations). 
3. Discussion needed : With E2E, BY, OTC and MDG teams
Impacted Teams: E2E, Master Data, OTC IF, SCP IF
